checked_sources: OpenStax Elementary Algebra 2e Ch 2.4–2.5 (Use a General Strategy to Solve Linear Equations / Solve Equations with Variables on Both Sides) reference listed in course JSON
Khan Academy Variables on Both Sides practice reference listed in course JSON
findings: Module title, order, and objective in script.json match the published Algebra I module 5 in server/prisma/courses/math/algebra-i.json: 'Solving Multi-Step Equations & Literal Equations'.

Assignment described in the lesson (10 multi-step equations with variables on both sides, parentheses, and fractions; 5 literal-equation rearrangements such as A = lw and PV = nRT, each with one sentence explaining the rearranged variable) matches the assignment string in the source packet.
Literal-equation examples used (A = lw and PV = nRT) are exactly the examples named in the source-of-truth module assignment, so the lesson stays bound to the published course.
No AP, College Board, Common App, CEEB, NCAA, accreditation, or admissions claims appear in lesson metadata, slide text, or narration.

No sourced or generated bitmap images are used; all precision visuals (concept card, worked traces, literal-equation rearrangements, compare slide, pause answer reveals) are drawn deterministically in build_slides.py — no assets_manifest.json required.
Narration refers to OpenStax and Khan Academy by name and never reads a URL aloud.
